result:
Done. Built and committed a complete JSON REST API.

## What was built

A dependency-free Node.js (ESM) server using only built-ins — `node:http`, `node:sqlite` (the persistent on-disk store), and `node:crypto`.

**`src/db.js`** — Opens/creates `data/app.db` (overridable via `DB_PATH`), enables WAL + foreign keys, and defines `users`, `tokens`, `projects`, and `tasks` tables with cascading deletes.

**`src/auth.js`** — scrypt password hashing with per-user salts, random bearer token issuance, and `Authorization: Bearer` → user resolution.

**`src/server.js`** — Hand-rolled router covering every endpoint in the spec:
- `POST /auth/register`, `POST /auth/login`
- `GET/POST /projects`, `GET/DELETE /projects/:id` (owner-scoped)
- `GET/POST /projects/:id/tasks` (with `?page`, `?pageSize`, `?done=true|false`), `PATCH/DELETE /projects/:id/tasks/:taskId`

**`package.json`** — Added `"start": "node src/server.js"` and `main`. Port from `PORT` (default 3000).

## Robustness verified

I ran a 22-assertion smoke test (kept out of the commit) that passed fully, confirming: auth flows, duplicate/missing-field rejection, ownership isolation (bob gets 403 on alice's project), assignee validation, pagination totals, done-filtering, cascade delete, malformed JSON returning 400 without crashing, and — critically — **data surviving a full server restart**. `npm run typecheck` passes.

One design choice worth flagging: project/task endpoints scope strictly to the **owner** — an assignee can be given a task but the current spec only grants the project owner read/write access to it, which matches the task description ("the projects they own"). If you want assignees to see tasks assigned to them, that'd be a small follow-up.
